preamble: The shift from the velar nasal [ŋ] to the bilabial nasal [m] is a process of labialization and fronting. This transformation typically occurs as regressive assimilation when a velar nasal is adjacent to a labial sound, or through an intermediate labial-velar [ŋ͡m] stage where the velar component is eventually lost.

examples.note: Regressive assimilation of the velar nasal before the bilabial stop [p] in rapid speech.
Historical shift often passing through a stable labial-velar nasal [ŋ͡m] stage (found in languages of Vanuatu).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
